evals.name: new-skill-placement
evals.prompt: I want to create a new skill that documents how we handle email sending in the project. Where should it go and what should the folder be named?

assertions.text: Proposes the path .claude/skills/vue-ssr-email/ (or similar kebab-case with vue-ssr- prefix)
States that the skill folder name must match the frontmatter name exactly
Mentions the domain prefix convention (vue-ssr-* for project-scoped skills)
Requires a SKILL.md file inside the folder
Recommends a references/ subdirectory if the skill exceeds ~200 lines
Mentions running scripts/audit.py after creation to validate

evals.name: claude-md-bloat-detection
evals.prompt: I added a 50-line section to CLAUDE.md explaining how the SSR lifecycle works step by step. Is that okay?

assertions.text: States the content does NOT belong in CLAUDE.md (it is knowledge, not a hard rule)
References the 10 KB budget
Recommends moving the content to a skill (e.g. vue-ssr-architecture)
Explains CLAUDE.md should only contain hard rules needed every turn
May suggest the content already exists in the vue-ssr-architecture skill

evals.name: agent-validation-anti-pattern
evals.prompt: I'm writing a new sub-agent for reviewing Vue components. In its instructions I want it to run 'npm run lint' to verify its own changes. Is that correct?

assertions.text: Clearly states this is an anti-pattern / violates the sub-agent contract
Explains that validation is centralized on the hooks agent only
States no sub-agent (except hooks) should run npm test/lint/format
Recommends removing the lint step from the sub-agent
References the orchestrator's responsibility to delegate to hooks at task end

evals.name: skill-description-quality
evals.prompt: I wrote a skill with this description: 'Helps with testing in the project.' — is this good enough?

assertions.text: States the description is too vague / not discriminating
Explains the description is the primary triggering mechanism
Recommends adding: framework (Vitest), file paths, trigger keywords
Recommends adding a Don't use for anti-trigger clause
Mentions the 80-char minimum length or 1536-char budget

evals.name: audit-run-after-change
evals.prompt: I just modified the vue-ssr-auth skill — changed its description and added a reference file. What should I do before considering this done?

assertions.text: Instructs to run scripts/audit.py
Mentions checking for broken links (new reference must be linked from SKILL.md)
Mentions verifying name/folder match and description length
Suggests walking the manual audit checklist
Reminds to propose corrections, never auto-fix

evals.name: rule-vs-skill-decision
evals.prompt: I want to make sure that whenever someone edits a file in src/api/, they always validate ObjectId. Should I create a skill for that or is there a better option?

assertions.text: Recommends a .claude/rules/ file (path-scoped rule) as the primary mechanism
Explains rules load deterministically by path (100% hit rate)
Notes this is a constraint/guardrail, not knowledge
May mention the existing objectid-validation.md rule already covers this
Mentions the complementary model: rule = guardrail, skill = knowledge

evals.name: rule-too-large
evals.prompt: I created a rule file in .claude/rules/ that's 4KB long — it documents the full SSR rendering pipeline with examples. Is that appropriate?

assertions.text: States that 4KB is too large for a rule (max ~2KB)
Explains rules should be lightweight constraints, not knowledge
Recommends extracting to a skill (vue-ssr-architecture already has this)
Suggests keeping only the core constraint in the rule
May reference anti-pattern D3 (rule too large)
